source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Synchronous slash command (no LLM call)
notes: OpenCode synchronous no-LLM slash command — TARGET spec; NOT yet recorded (driver_gap: slash-command). OpenCode's Source is ProcessOwnedStore (SQLite, core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/agent.go): a transient proc-<PID> pre-session is born ready at launch, then a SQLite-keyed ses_… session appears in ready and runs one warm-up turn (ready->working->ready). The warm-up turn is needed so the store row exists for resolve_transcript to bind. After warm_turn_end returns to ready, the slash_window phase pins the ses_… session ready with duration_at_least_ms:4000 and invariants forbidding state_transition to working/waiting — those are what would fire if the local slash (incorrectly) drove a model call. A no-LLM slash writes no new assistant turn to the store, so the session stays ready (the read-from-store no-op). KNOWN GAP: this cell is by_adapter.opencode.applicable:false — driver_capability:gap:slash-command. drive-opencode-interactive.sh drives BLOCKING headless `opencode run` subprocesses (case "$TYPE" handles only send|wait_turn|sleep) with NO step to deliver an in-TUI slash command; CONFIRMED THIS SWEEP that headless `opencode run` does NOT process in-TUI slash commands — it stores them as literal user text (proven with inline /model and /new), so `opencode run "/help"` would run a model turn (ready->working->ready), inverting this scenario's premise. The slash_window phase below describes the LIVE-observable arc a live-REPL opencode driver would produce, but is not yet validated against a committed recording. Recording deferred until the opencode interactive driver gains a live-REPL slash-command primitive — a developer task, out of scope for the implement stage.

text: Pre-session row (proc-<PID>) appears in ready within 2s of opencode launch
Warm-up prompt drives ready -> working on the SQLite-keyed ses_… session (materializes the store row so subsequent steps can resolve_transcript)
Warm-up turn completes back to ready (same ses_… session id)
Spec: state stays ready throughout the no-LLM slash command — no working transition driven by the synchronous local slash (the store row gains no new assistant turn)
